title: Race-lap ranking specialist instead of only global binary AUC models
summary: Pit calls are relative decisions within a small same-lap candidate set.
rationale: Pit calls are relative decisions within a small same-lap candidate set. CatBoost ranking supports `GroupId`/pairwise objectives, and within a `Race-Year-LapNumber` group the useful question is often which drivers are most pit-likely now, not just each row’s absolute probability. This complements current threshold-style features.
implementation_hint: Train a second model with group id = `Race_Year x LapNumber` (or `Race x Year x LapNumber`) and labels = `PitNextLap`, using CatBoost ranking loss (`YetiRankPairwise`/`PairLogitPairwise`). Blend its OOF ranks with the existing classifier. Add only leakage-safe within-group relative features such as percentile TyreLife, percentile LapTime_Delta, and gap from group medians.
expected_effect: Better ordering among same-lap contenders, improving ROC AUC even if absolute calibration is unchanged.
risk: If many groups have zero or one positive, ranking can be noisy; grouping too coarsely can dilute signal, and fold leakage is easy if group statistics use future rows.
